coin_profile.name: Janus Henderson Anemoy Treasury Fund
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Janus Henderson Anemoy Treasury Fund (JTRSY) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #74 by market cap.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: JTRSY demonstrates minimal competitive defensibility with no apparent technological differentiation or switching costs, and the protocol shows weak network effects typical of cash-flow protocols where user growth doesn't inherently benefit existing participants. While dilution control scores perfectly (10), the fundamental weakness across fee growth, capital efficiency, holder value accrual, and network demand (all 5/10) indicates limited real economic value capture for token holders and low ecosystem lock-in, placing it below governance tokens like ARB/OP due to lacking even their L2 positioning advantages.

chart_zones.mos_note: Cycle phase (Insufficient Data) not favourable for entry — wait for accumulation or early uptrend.

2026.net_issuance_note: Current snapshot only. Historical supply data requires on-chain indexing not available via free APIs.

meta.data_note: No protocol fee or TVL data found for this token on DeFiLlama. Charts 1 and 2 are unavailable. Charts 3 and 4 show current-year supply and dilution data from CoinGecko.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 5.3/10 and survivability 63.3%. Base: 7.0% p.a., Bull: 13.0% p.a., Bear: -21.1% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (7.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (5.3/10). Bull (13.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-21.1%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 63.3% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Only 1 method contributed to the median — the valuation should be treated as a rough anchor, not a precise fair value. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

coin_profile.name: JUST
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: JUST (JST) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #80 by market cap.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: JST (TRON's governance token) has minimal competitive defensibility as TRON itself is a forked L1 competing against stronger chains, with low switching costs for users and developers. Token utility is weak—holders capture limited economic value from protocol cash flows, evidenced by poor holder_value_accrual (5/10) and network_demand (4/10) scores, while high dilution control (8/10) cannot offset fundamental protocol-level moats. Ecosystem lock-in is shallow compared to Ethereum or Solana, regulatory resilience is uncertain, and the protocol lacks the 15-year security track record or irreplaceable infrastructure that would justify a higher moat score.

chart_zones.mos_note: Price above speculative fair value — no margin of safety.

watchlist_signal.reason: Weak fundamentals or heavily overextended price relative to value — higher-quality opportunities available elsewhere.

bull_points: <cite index="1-1,1-10">JustLend DAO completed Phase 1 deflationary burns (559M JST in October 2025) with $41M earmarked for phased burns through 2026, creating immediate supply reduction that could reduce selling pressure as scarce token supply compounds.</cite>

<cite index="1-12,1-18,1-19">Multi-chain expansion to Ethereum and BNB Chain planned for Q2 2026 reduces reliance on TRON and diversifies liquidity sources—a concrete catalyst for ecosystem diversification that could attract new users and institutional capital.</cite>

<cite index="1-14,1-15,1-16">DAO governance activation in Q1 2026 will empower JST holders to vote on protocol parameters and upgrades, increasing token utility and holder engagement as decentralization accelerates community participation.
bear_points: <cite index="1-4,1-5,1-6">The near-complete liquidation of USDJ (95% of supply redeemed by August 2025) removes a core revenue driver for JST governance, potentially eroding investor confidence as the protocol's economic moat weakens.</cite>

<cite index="1-23">Revenue-dependent JST burns carry execution risk—a drop in TRON DeFi activity or stablecoin adoption would immediately reduce the $41M allocated for phased burns through 2026, creating deflationary expectations miss.

<cite index="1-25">Governance participation risks and revenue volatility remain structural concerns for JST's near-term stability, with execution challenges from slow DAO participation potentially delaying protocol upgrades and ecosystem development.

bull_points: <cite index="1-9,1-20,1-21,1-22">Revenue-driven quarterly burns use 30% of JustLend protocol profits, and with the fixed 9.9B total JST supply, sustained profitability creates structural deflationary dynamics that compound holder value over 3-5 years as circulating supply contracts.</cite>

<cite index="1-29,1-30">TRON's 52% energy cost reduction and improved infrastructure accessibility, combined with JST's Biconomy listing, position the token to capture ecosystem growth as lower transaction fees remove user adoption barriers long-term.

<cite index="1-2,1-28">With JST fully circulating since 2023 and supply fixed at 9.9B, sustained revenue-funded burns create scarcity mechanics that, if demand holds, should amplify long-term token appreciation across market cycles.
bear_points: No historical multi-year fee data exists for JUST; the protocol's long-term economic durability and cash-flow sustainability remain unproven over 3-5 years, making the deflationary burn thesis speculative without proven revenue consistency.

<cite index="1-23">TRON DeFi adoption volatility presents existential risk—if ecosystem activity declines materially, protocol revenue collapse would eliminate the $41M+ burn capacity, destroying the core long-term deflationary value proposition.

<cite index="1-25">Structural execution risks including contentious governance proposals and voter apathy could slow protocol development and ecosystem investment, while regulatory disruption to TRON or stablecoin frameworks poses unquantifiable 5-year downside risk to JST's competitive position.

coin_profile.name: Jupiter
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Jupiter is Solana's dominant DEX aggregator, routing trades across all Solana DEXes for best execution. It handles the majority of Solana's swap volume. JUP token holders receive fee distributions, and the protocol is central to Solana's DeFi infrastructure.

JUP.name: Jupiter

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: JUP is a DEX aggregator with strong capital efficiency and fee growth, but lacks meaningful moat defensibility: the core routing logic can be easily forked (as seen with competitors), network effects are weak since users optimize purely for price execution, and switching costs are negligible. Token utility is real but limited—JUP fee accrual to holders is strong (8/10), but dilution control is severely lacking (2/10 circulating supply), and the protocol's maturity (4/10) suggests it remains a growth-stage product vulnerable to being commoditized as Solana's DEX landscape consolidates.

chart_zones.mos_note: Margin of safety present — price near or below speculative fair value during accumulation (deep bear) phase.

forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Fee-based methods (Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple) used DeFiLlama protocol fee data to anchor the valuation. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Metcalfe Network Value is shown in the breakdown chart without contributing to the median: the implied IV is below 10% of current price, indicating the token is trading at a significant premium to its network activity level. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment penalises the large gap between circulating and fully-diluted supply — future token unlocks are expected to create selling pressure. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Metcalfe Network Value is shown in the breakdown chart without contributing to the median: the implied IV is below 10% of current price, indicating the token is trading at a significant premium to its network activity level. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Only 2 methods contributed to the median. More data sources (DeFiLlama fee history) would improve confidence in this estimate. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

coin_profile.name: Jito
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Jito (JTO) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #169 by market cap.

JTO.name: Jito

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: JTO has minimal protocol moat due to weak competitive defensibility (Jito Labs' MEV-Boost infrastructure can be replicated), lack of meaningful network effects beyond SOL's validator ecosystem, and severely limited token utility with only 47.57% circulating supply and poor dilution control (score 2). While network demand scores well (10) by riding Solana's throughput, the token captures minimal holder value, has no ecosystem lock-in advantages, and faces regulatory uncertainty as a MEV extraction layer—making it vulnerable to both technical forking and regulatory pressure that could rapidly erode its marginal position.
